questions.q: By what title was Lala Lajpat Rai popularly known?
questions.explanation: Lala Lajpat Rai was widely celebrated as the Lion of Punjab (Punjab Kesari) for his fiery nationalist leadership.

questions.q: Lala Lajpat Rai was one of which famous nationalist trio?
questions.explanation: He formed the assertive nationalist trio Lal-Bal-Pal along with Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Bipin Chandra Pal.

questions.q: While protesting against which commission was Lala Lajpat Rai fatally injured in 1928?
questions.explanation: He was injured during a lathi charge while leading a protest against the all-British Simon Commission in 1928.

questions.q: How was Lala Lajpat Rai injured during the anti-Simon Commission protest?
options: In a lathi charge by police
questions.explanation: He suffered severe injuries in a police lathi charge while protesting the Simon Commission's arrival in Lahore.

questions.q: In which province was Lala Lajpat Rai a leading nationalist figure?
questions.explanation: He was the foremost nationalist leader of Punjab, earning the epithet Lion of Punjab.

questions.q: Apart from being a nationalist, Lala Lajpat Rai was also a noted what?
questions.explanation: He was a prominent social reformer associated with the Arya Samaj movement and educational work.

questions.q: Lala Lajpat Rai belonged to which wing of the nationalist movement?
questions.explanation: Along with Tilak and Pal, he led the assertive or 'Extremist' wing that pressed for bolder political action.

questions.q: In which year was Lala Lajpat Rai fatally injured during the Simon Commission protest?
questions.explanation: The lathi charge that led to his death occurred in 1928 during the anti-Simon Commission demonstrations.

questions.q: With which religious reform movement was Lala Lajpat Rai closely associated?
questions.explanation: He was a devoted member of the Arya Samaj founded by Swami Dayananda Saraswati.

questions.q: Lala Lajpat Rai's death is often linked to the later revenge action carried out by which revolutionary?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh and his comrades sought to avenge Lajpat Rai's death by targeting a police officer in Lahore.

questions.q: In which year was Lala Lajpat Rai born?
questions.explanation: Lala Lajpat Rai was born on 28 January 1865 in Dhudike, Punjab.

questions.q: In 1907, the British deported Lala Lajpat Rai without trial to which place?
questions.explanation: He was deported to Mandalay in Burma in 1907 for his agitation in Punjab over agrarian unrest.

questions.q: Which financial institution did Lala Lajpat Rai help found in 1894?
questions.explanation: He was among the founders of Punjab National Bank, established in 1894 in Lahore.

questions.q: Which famous words are attributed to Lala Lajpat Rai after he was struck during the protest?
options: Every blow on me will be a nail in the coffin of British rule
Give me blood and I will give you freedom
questions.explanation: He declared that every blow he received would be a nail in the coffin of the British Empire.

questions.q: Lala Lajpat Rai presided over a special session of the Indian National Congress in which year?
questions.explanation: He presided over the special Congress session at Calcutta in 1920, which endorsed the Non-Cooperation Movement.

questions.q: Which English-language weekly did Lala Lajpat Rai found to spread nationalist ideas?
questions.explanation: He founded the weekly 'The People' to articulate nationalist views in English.

questions.q: In which city did the lathi charge that fatally injured Lala Lajpat Rai take place?
questions.explanation: The Simon Commission protest and the resulting lathi charge took place in Lahore on 30 October 1928.

questions.q: Lala Lajpat Rai spent time touring and lecturing for India's cause primarily in which country during World War I?
questions.explanation: He stayed in the United States from 1914, where he founded the Indian Home Rule League of America in 1917.

questions.q: Which educational and welfare organization did Lala Lajpat Rai help promote and lead in Punjab?
options: The Servants of the People Society
questions.explanation: He founded the Servants of the People Society (Lok Sevak Mandal) in 1921 to train workers for social and national service.

questions.q: Which influential book on his American observations did Lala Lajpat Rai write?
options: The United States of America: A Hindu's Impressions
questions.explanation: He wrote 'The United States of America: A Hindu's Impressions and a Study' during his years abroad.

questions.q: By profession before politics, Lala Lajpat Rai practised as a what?
questions.explanation: He trained and practised as a lawyer before devoting himself fully to nationalist work.

questions.q: In 1907, Lala Lajpat Rai and which Punjab leader were deported together over agrarian agitation?
questions.explanation: He was deported to Mandalay alongside Sardar Ajit Singh, an uncle of Bhagat Singh, in 1907.

questions.q: On which date in 1928 did Lala Lajpat Rai die from his injuries?
questions.explanation: He was injured on 30 October 1928 and died of his wounds on 17 November 1928.

questions.q: Which British police officer's actions during the Lahore lathi charge were blamed for Lala Lajpat Rai's death?
questions.explanation: Superintendent James A. Scott ordered and led the lathi charge; revolutionaries had intended to target him in revenge.

questions.q: In the revenge action for Lala Lajpat Rai's death, Bhagat Singh's group mistakenly shot which officer instead of their intended target?
questions.explanation: Mistaking him for Scott, the revolutionaries shot Assistant Superintendent John Saunders in December 1928.

questions.q: In which Punjab village was Lala Lajpat Rai born?
questions.explanation: He was born in 1865 in the village of Dhudike, in present-day Moga district of Punjab (historically part of the larger Ferozepur region).

questions.q: Lala Lajpat Rai authored a notable biography of which Italian nationalist revolutionary?
questions.explanation: Inspired by European nationalism, he wrote a biography of Giuseppe Mazzini, among other historical works.

questions.q: Lala Lajpat Rai was a member of the Central Legislative Assembly representing which group from 1926?
questions.explanation: He was elected to the Central Legislative Assembly in the 1920s as a nationalist associated with the Swarajist bloc.

questions.q: Which educational network in Punjab did Lala Lajpat Rai actively support as an Arya Samajist?
questions.explanation: He was a strong patron of the DAV (Dayanand Anglo-Vedic) educational movement linked to the Arya Samaj.

questions.q: Lala Lajpat Rai's pamphlet criticizing British rule that led to his 1907 deportation focused on unrest among which group?
options: Punjab peasants and the colonization bills
questions.explanation: His agitation over the Punjab Colonization Bill and canal water rates among peasants prompted the British to deport him in 1907.
